reachability_artifacts: sworn run --parallel --release <fixture> with a real injected merge failure, recording non-zero exit, terminal output, supervisor row, status revision, Git ref, and lifecycle event
sworn run --task <fixture> with a post-acquire failure, recording non-zero exit and failed supervisor release

effort_complexity.rationale: See spec.json; the implementer must reassess failure ordering against live finalizers before design review.

release_benefit: A SwornAgent operator can trust a recoverable autonomous release loop, receive durable attention pages, and monitor or safely control it from a mobile web board.

positive_scenarios: A fully persisted verified slice returns PASS with status, Git, and event references.
A successful track merge records done only after its Git effect exists.
negative_scenarios: A status, proof, Git, supervisor, or event failure cannot return the intended terminal label.
Ready-to-merge cannot be reported as a completed integration merge.
validation.benefit_hypothesis: Making terminal claims evidence-bearing removes false completion and gives later recovery and monitoring one truthful boundary.

design_decisions.choice: Use ordered durable effects plus reconciliation rather than claim a cross-store ACID transaction.
design_decisions.rationale: Git, Baton files, and SQLite cannot share one atomic commit, so terminal publication must prove all required references.

design_decisions.choice: Keep ready_to_merge distinct from merge success.
options: readiness is a separate non-merge result
design_decisions.rationale: Operational autonomy does not imply delegated production merge authority.
